meta.title: In-Force Customer Service
meta.catalog: Salesforce Service Cloud — Insurance Servicing Best-Practice Catalog

steps.activity: Inbound contact received (phone, email, chat, portal)
steps.output: Contact logged in CRM; channel and timestamp recorded
3-point verification: policy number, DOB and postcode/email; on hold until passed
Contact type coded in CRM (enquiry, update, payment, document, complaint)
Policy status, payment standing and interaction history reviewed before engaging

steps.activity: Standard enquiry resolved at first contact
steps.output: Policy info, billing, payment dates and coverage summaries within CSR authority
Update processed in PAS; confirmation emailed; change noted in CRM
Certificate, schedule or receipt regenerated and emailed within 24 hours

steps.activity: Payment received or arrangement set up
steps.output: Payment via IVR or agent-assisted; arrangement per SOP-INS-003 authority

steps.activity: Complex or sensitive contact escalated to Member Care
steps.output: Escalation logged; Member Care contacts policyholder within 2 hours
Summary of actions communicated verbally or in writing

steps.activity: Contact record closed and coded in CRM
steps.output: Resolution code, handle time and CSAT prompt recorded; follow-up task if pending
CSAT survey dispatched; responses captured for quality reporting

swim_phases.name: Contact receipt & verification
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–4 · intake to context
swim_phases.note: Every contact passes 3-point identity verification before any account information is provided; failures trigger a security flag.

swim_phases.name: Request handling
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 5–9 · resolve, update, escalate
swim_phases.note: Standard enquiries, updates, documents and payments are handled at first contact; complex or sensitive cases escalate to Member Care.

swim_phases.name: Closure & quality
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 10–12 · confirm to survey
swim_phases.note: Contacts are confirmed, coded and closed, and a CSAT survey is dispatched for quality reporting.

fitgap.summary_line: Overall alignment of SOP-INS-009 against the Salesforce Service Cloud servicing catalog
detail_slides: Steps 1–4 vs Salesforce Service Cloud catalog
Steps 5–11 vs Salesforce Service Cloud catalog
Steps 12–13 vs Salesforce Service Cloud catalog

items.name: Omni-channel intake
items.detail: Phone, email, chat and portal logged in CRM with channel/timestamp align with Service Cloud omni-channel.

items.name: Identity verification
items.detail: 3-point knowledge-based verification is enforced, but the catalog recommends step-up authentication (OTP/biometric) for sensitive changes — KBA alone is increasingly bypassable.

items.name: Contact categorisation
items.detail: Coded contact types in CRM align with Service Cloud case classification.

items.name: Unified policy context
items.detail: Policy context is reviewed from PAS, but the agent works across PAS and CRM without a single 360° customer view/agent desktop as the catalog recommends.

items.name: First-contact resolution
items.detail: Standard enquiries resolved within authority at first contact align with Service Cloud FCR practice.

items.name: Self-service deflection
items.detail: A self-service portal exists but is limited; the catalog deflects routine contacts via an AI assistant/chatbot and expanded self-service, reducing volume and AHT.

items.name: AI agent-assist & knowledge
items.detail: No agent-assist, knowledge suggestions or next-best-action. The catalog surfaces knowledge and recommended actions in-flow to improve consistency and FCR.

items.name: Account update processing
items.detail: PAS account updates with emailed confirmation align with Service Cloud service requests.

items.name: Escalation to Member Care
items.detail: Logged escalation with a 2-hour callback aligns with Service Cloud escalation/queue management.

items.name: Complaint & sentiment detection
items.detail: Complaints are spotted by manual keyword recognition. The catalog applies automated sentiment/complaint detection to flag at-risk interactions in real time.

items.name: Vulnerable-customer & consent handling
items.detail: No structured vulnerable-customer identification or consent capture for changes. The catalog supports vulnerability flags and consent logging — a conduct and privacy gap.

items.name: Closure coding & CSAT
items.detail: Resolution coding, handle-time capture and CSAT survey align with Service Cloud closure and feedback.

items.name: Quality / interaction analytics
items.detail: Quality is scored manually on samples. The catalog uses interaction/speech analytics to QA at scale and coach automatically.

critical_missing.title: Step-up authentication
critical_missing.detail: Identity relies on 3-point knowledge-based verification, increasingly bypassable. The catalog adds step-up authentication (OTP/biometric) for sensitive changes to control account-takeover fraud.

critical_missing.title: AI agent-assist & self-service deflection
critical_missing.detail: No agent-assist, knowledge or next-best-action, and limited self-service. The catalog deflects routine contacts via an AI assistant and surfaces knowledge in-flow, improving FCR and lowering AHT.

critical_missing.title: Vulnerable-customer & consent handling
critical_missing.detail: No structured vulnerable-customer identification or consent capture for changes, a conduct and privacy gap.

critical_missing.title: Interaction / speech analytics for QA
critical_missing.detail: Quality is scored on manual samples; the catalog QA's interactions at scale with speech/interaction analytics and automated coaching.

control_bars.name: Omni-channel intake
control_bars.note: Phone, email, chat, portal logged in CRM

control_bars.name: Unified 360 view
control_bars.note: PAS + CRM not unified into one desktop

control_bars.name: Self-service / AI assist
control_bars.note: Limited portal; no AI assist or chatbot

control_bars.name: Vulnerable-customer handling
control_bars.note: No vulnerability flags or consent logging

remediations.title: Add step-up authentication for sensitive changes
remediations.detail: Require OTP or biometric step-up beyond 3-point KBA for payment, bank and contact changes to control account takeover. Effort: 4 weeks.

remediations.title: Deploy AI agent-assist & self-service deflection
remediations.detail: Add an AI assistant/chatbot for routine contacts and surface knowledge and next-best-action in the agent desktop to improve FCR and AHT. Effort: 7 weeks.

remediations.title: Add vulnerable-customer & consent handling
remediations.detail: Introduce vulnerability identification flags and consent capture/logging for account changes. Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Unify the agent desktop (360 view)
remediations.detail: Surface a single 360° customer view across PAS and CRM to cut context switching. Effort: 5 weeks.

remediations.title: Add interaction/speech analytics for QA
remediations.detail: QA interactions at scale with speech/interaction analytics and automated complaint/sentiment detection and coaching. Effort: 5 weeks.

opt_phases.name: Request handling & assist
opt_phases.subtitle: Deflect, assist, resolve, escalate
opt_phases.roles: CSR · Policy Service · Member Care · AI

3-point KBA plus OTP/biometric step-up for sensitive changes
Intent classified automatically; type coded in CRM
Single 360° view across PAS and CRM presented to the agent
Vulnerability flags applied; consent captured for any change
Routine requests deflected to chatbot/self-service before agent handling
Knowledge and next-best-action surfaced in-flow; resolved at first contact
Update in PAS with consent; confirmation emailed
Certificate, schedule or receipt regenerated within 24 hours

opt_steps.activity: Payment received or arrangement set up
opt_steps.output: Payment via IVR/assisted; arrangement per SOP-INS-003

opt_steps.activity: Complex / sensitive escalated to Member Care
Contact closed and coded in CRM
opt_steps.output: Resolution code, handle time and CSAT prompt
Interaction/speech analytics QA every contact; CSAT captured; coaching automated

opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–5 · v3.1 adds step-up & 360 view
opt_swim_phases.note: v3.1 adds step-up authentication, an AI-classified intent, a unified 360 view, and vulnerability/consent capture.

opt_swim_phases.name: Request handling & assist
opt_swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 6–11 · deflection and agent-assist
opt_swim_phases.note: v3.1 deflects routine contacts to self-service and gives agents knowledge and next-best-action; sentiment flags drive escalation.

opt_swim_phases.note: v3.1 QA's every interaction with speech/interaction analytics and captures CSAT for automated coaching.

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Salesforce Service Cloud Insurance Servicing Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v3.1

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-009. Alignment to the Salesforce Service Cloud servicing catalog increased from 69% to 91%. Critical additions: step-up authentication, AI agent-assist with self-service deflection, vulnerable-customer and consent handling, a unified 360 view, and interaction/speech analytics for quality.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the Service Cloud catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional automation or controls

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines how inbound requests from current policyholders are received and resolved across all channels. Version 3.1 incorporates optimisations from fit-gap analysis against the Salesforce Service Cloud Insurance Servicing Best-Practice Catalog, achieving 91% alignment (up from 69% in v3.0).

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to inbound policyholder contacts for general enquiries, policy information, billing queries, payment processing, account updates and document requests.
Scope extended: step-up authentication and vulnerable-customer/consent handling now apply to every sensitive request.
This procedure does NOT cover claims notifications (SOP-INS-007/011/012), formal complaints (SOP-COMP-001), or policy cancellation (SOP-INS-011).

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Logs contacts; verifies identity; resolves standard requests with agent-assist; escalates.
Handles complex/sensitive and VIP cases; owns vulnerable-customer handling.
Processes formal account changes in PAS; back-office support.
Monitors queues; handles escalations; reviews analytics-driven quality.

sections.title: 5. Identity & Conduct Controls (New Section — v3.1)
blocks: New section. Required by the Service Cloud catalog. Step-up authentication and vulnerable-customer/consent handling now gate sensitive requests.

sections.title: 6. Process Steps
blocks: Payment received or arrangement set up
Contact closed and coded in CRM

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Step-up authentication strengthens identity assurance; repeated failures still trigger a security flag.
After 2 failed attempts, the CSR terminates the contact and advises a call-back with correct information.
A third failure within 24 hours triggers a security flag and Team Leader notification.
No account information is provided to an unverified caller under any circumstances.
Automated sentiment/complaint detection or agent recognition routes the contact to Member Care immediately.
Formal complaint logged in the Complaints Register within 30 minutes per SOP-COMP-001.
CSR must not attempt to resolve a formal complaint without Member Care or Supervisor involvement.
CSR transfers to Member Care and flags the policy as 'Security Hold' in PAS.
Fraud team notified within 1 hour; no account changes processed until fraud review is complete.

sections.title: 8. Systems and Tools
blocks: Contact logging, case management, 360 view, analytics

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v3.1: step-up authentication, consent logging, AI assist, and interaction analytics.
Identity assurance: 3-point verification plus step-up for sensitive changes; no service without verification.
Consent: account changes captured against logged policyholder consent.
Vulnerability: vulnerable customers identified and handled with appropriate care.
AI assist: knowledge and next-best-action surfaced in-flow for consistency.
Deflection: routine contacts handled via self-service to reduce volume.
Quality: every interaction QA'd via analytics with automated coaching.
Immutable audit trail: identity, consent, change and escalation events logged.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Full rewrite aligned to Salesforce CRM migration; new FCR and AHT KPI baselines
OPTIMISED: step-up authentication; AI agent-assist & self-service deflection; vulnerable-customer & consent handling; unified 360 view; interaction analytics; catalog alignment 69% → 91%
